solution.answer: $H_0 \cong \mathbb{Z},\quad H_2 \cong \mathbb{Z},\quad H_4 \cong \mathbb{Z},\quad H_k = 0 \text{ for } k \text{ odd or } k \geq 5.$
steps: $\mathbb{C}P^2$ is a compact 4-dimensional manifold with CW structure: one 0-cell, one 2-cell, one 4-cell.
Chain complex: $0 \to C_4 \xrightarrow{d_4} C_2 \xrightarrow{d_2} C_0 \to 0$, all other $C_k = 0$.
There are no adjacent cells (e.g.\ no 3-cells or 1-cells), so all boundary maps are trivially zero.
$H_0 = H_2 = H_4 = \mathbb{Z}$; all odd-dimensional homology and $H_k$ for $k \geq 5$ vanish.
$\mathbb{C}P^2$ is a closed orientable 4-manifold: consistent with $H_4 = \mathbb{Z}$.
